proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 80 binary features, at least one has a statistically significant univariate association with objective_response (chi-square test of independence with the binary outcome).
feature_035 = 1 patients have a higher objective_response rate than feature_035 = 0 patients (positive association).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square screen of all 80 binary features. The strongest signal was feature_035 (ORR 0.221 if =1 vs 0.165 if =0, diff +0.056, p=3.5e-18, n_pos=3624). Other nominally-significant binary features at p<0.05 included feature_093 (+0.017, p=5.6e-3), feature_121 (-0.014, p=0.025), and feature_014 (+0.007, p=0.031). The vast majority of binary features showed weak (|diff|<0.01) and non-significant associations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 37 continuous features, at least one has a statistically significant difference in mean values between objective responders and non-responders (Welch t-test).
feature_011 has lower mean values among objective responders than non-responders (negative association with response).
feature_006 has lower mean values among objective responders than non-responders (negative association with response).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_011'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_011'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 mean was 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (diff -0.57, t-test p=4.6e-35). Strongest continuous signal in the dataset.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_006'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_006'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 mean was 58.23 in responders vs 60.31 in non-responders (diff -2.08, t-test p=1.0e-19). Range of feature_006 was 20-100 with mean 60, plausibly age in years.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,c], df.loc[y==0,c])
analyses.result_summary: Other continuous features with significant univariate associations: feature_099 (+0.029, p=1.3e-6, positive), feature_063 (-0.51, p=2.9e-6, negative), feature_092 (-1.33, p=1.8e-5, negative), feature_084 (+1.17, p=0.048, weak positive). Most other continuous features were non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal: 0, 1, 2) shows a strong association with objective_response, with response rate decreasing monotonically across levels 0 -> 1 -> 2.
feature_005 (self-reported race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis.
feature_087 (insurance: medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['objective_response'].mean(); stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_057'], y))
analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_057: level 0 = 0.213 (n=17592), level 1 = 0.155 (n=24971), level 2 = 0.112 (n=7437). Chi-square p=5.5e-98. Largest single signal in the data.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], y))
analyses.result_summary: ORR by race: white=0.173 (n=32498), hispanic=0.167, asian=0.163, black=0.157, other=0.147. Chi-square p=9.6e-3 (significant in unadjusted analysis). White vs non-white absolute difference roughly 1.0 percentage point.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_087'], y))
analyses.result_summary: ORR by insurance: medicare=0.172, medicaid=0.169, private=0.167, uninsured=0.166. Chi-square p=0.54 (not significant). Insurance type is not associated with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All three pairwise differences in objective_response between feature_057 strata (0 vs 1, 0 vs 2, 1 vs 2) are statistically significant with response rate decreasing as feature_057 level increases.

analyses.code: for (a,b) in [(0,1),(0,2),(1,2)]: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_057']==a,'objective_response'], df.loc[df['feature_057']==b,'objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0 vs 1: ORR 0.213 vs 0.155, diff +0.058, p=6.5e-53. feature_057=0 vs 2: 0.213 vs 0.112, diff +0.101, p=3.7e-80. feature_057=1 vs 2: 0.155 vs 0.112, diff +0.043, p=1.3e-20. All three pairwise contrasts highly significant; the ordinal trend is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on the top univariate predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_035, feature_099, feature_093, feature_084, feature_121, feature_014, feature_005), each of feature_057 (negative ordinal), feature_011 (negative), feature_006 (negative), feature_035 (positive), and feature_099 (positive) retains an independently significant adjusted association with response.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit(); X has feature_057 dummies, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_035, feature_099, feature_093, feature_084, feature_121, feature_014, feature_005 dummies.
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic (n=50000, pseudo-R2=0.019, LLR p=4.7e-174). Adjusted log-OR coefficients: feature_057_1=-0.385 (p=2e-50), feature_057_2=-0.768 (p=2e-77), feature_011=-0.040/unit (p=2e-34), feature_006=-0.006/unit (p=1e-19), feature_063=-0.007/unit (p=1.5e-6), feature_092=-0.002/unit (p=1e-5), feature_035=+0.360 (OR 1.43, p=2e-17), feature_099=+0.118/unit (p=1e-6), feature_093=+0.126 (p=0.003), feature_121=-0.089 (p=0.049), feature_014=+0.059 (p=0.016). Race dummies all p>0.13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 shows a roughly monotonic dose-response relationship with objective_response, with ORR decreasing by approximately 5 percentage points from the lowest decile to the highest decile of feature_006.

analyses.code: df['f006_dec'] = pd.qcut(df['feature_006'], 10); df.groupby('f006_dec')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: ORR across feature_006 deciles is monotonically decreasing: lowest decile (20-34) = 0.194, decile 5 (55-60) = 0.167, top decile (85-100) = 0.143. Logistic OR per unit increase = 0.994 (p=1.2e-19). Consistent with roughly linear age-like effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 shows a roughly monotonic negative dose-response relationship within nonzero values, but a substantial fraction (~28%) of the cohort has feature_011 == 0, and these patients have an ORR that is higher than the lowest non-zero quintile, suggesting feature_011 = 0 is a special category rather than the bottom of a continuous range.

analyses.code: df['f011_zero'] = (df['feature_011']==0); plus quintile breakdown of nonzero subset
analyses.result_summary: 27.7% of patients have feature_011 == 0 (n=13850, ORR=0.186). Among patients with feature_011 > 0 (n=36150, ORR=0.162), the response rate decreases monotonically across quintiles: Q1 (0.1-2.0) ORR=0.186, Q2 (2.0-3.9) 0.175, Q3 (3.9-5.8) 0.166, Q4 (5.8-8.3) 0.155, Q5 (8.3-23.6) 0.129. Within the nonzero subset, logistic coef = -0.045 per unit (p=3.2e-27). The zero patients behave like the lowest nonzero quintile (Q1 ORR matches), supporting a continuous effect rather than a separate zero category.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The distribution of feature_057 strata (0/1/2) is essentially identical across racial groups (within ~2 percentage points), indicating that feature_057 is not patterned by race.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], df['feature_057'], normalize='index')
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 distribution by race is nearly uniform: white (35.1/50.1/14.9), black (35.0/50.2/14.8), hispanic (35.2/49.9/14.9), asian (36.5/48.4/15.1), other (35.5/49.6/14.9). No meaningful patterning of biology by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, and feature_035, insurance type (feature_087) has no significant residual association with objective_response.

analyses.code: Logistic regression of y on insurance dummies + biology adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients: medicare +0.022 (p=0.56), private -0.020 (p=0.59), uninsured -0.012 (p=0.86). All non-significant. Insurance does not predict objective_response in either unadjusted or adjusted analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, and feature_035, the previously significant univariate race difference in ORR (feature_005) attenuates to non-significance for all categories.

analyses.code: Logistic of y on race dummies + biology adjustment (feature_057 dummies, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR (vs asian reference): black -0.029 (p=0.64), hispanic +0.041 (p=0.49), other -0.116 (p=0.26), white +0.078 (p=0.13). The unadjusted ~1 ppt advantage for white patients is fully explained by other measured covariates and is no longer detectable after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no statistically significant interaction between race (feature_005) and biology stratum (feature_057) on objective_response: feature_057 effects are uniform across racial groups.

analyses.code: LR test of full race x feature_057 interaction model vs main-effects model
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio chi-square = 3.39 on 8 df, p=0.91. ORRs within each feature_057 stratum are very similar across races (e.g., feature_057=0: white 0.217, asian 0.204, black 0.205, hispanic 0.209). No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_035 effect on objective_response is consistent in direction and magnitude across age (feature_006) tertiles; there is no significant feature_035 x feature_006 interaction.

analyses.code: stratified chi-square within feature_006 tertiles, plus logistic with f035 x f006 interaction term
analyses.result_summary: feature_035 effect by age tertile: low (20-50) +4.2 ppt (p=2.8e-4), mid (50-70) +7.8 ppt (p=6.8e-12), high (70-100) +4.9 ppt (p=5.1e-6). Interaction coefficient feature_035*feature_006 = +0.0019 per unit, p=0.39. Effect direction and magnitude broadly consistent; no formal interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative feature_011 effect on objective_response is consistent in direction across feature_057 strata; there is no significant feature_011 x feature_057 interaction.

analyses.code: logistic regression within each feature_057 stratum, plus LR test of interaction
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 logistic coefficients within stratum: feature_057=0 coef -0.041 (p=5e-16); feature_057=1 -0.037 (p=1e-14); feature_057=2 -0.052 (p=5e-7). LR test of feature_011 x feature_057 interaction: chi-square 1.91 on 2 df, p=0.39. Effect is uniformly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative feature_006 effect on objective_response is consistent across feature_057 strata; there is no significant feature_006 x feature_057 interaction.

analyses.code: LR test of feature_006 x feature_057 interaction in logistic regression
analyses.result_summary: LR test chi-square = 1.42 on 2 df, p=0.49. No evidence that the feature_006 (likely age) effect on response varies by biology stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The top continuous predictors (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, feature_084) are mutually uncorrelated (|r| < 0.05 for all pairs), so each contributes independent information about response.

analyses.code: df[top_cont].corr()
analyses.result_summary: All pairwise Pearson correlations among the top six continuous predictors fell within |r| < 0.01 (e.g., feature_011 vs feature_006 r=-0.005; feature_011 vs feature_063 r=-0.008; feature_006 vs feature_092 r=-0.001). The continuous biomarkers are essentially orthogonal, consistent with the multivariable model where each retained an independent significant coefficient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the six top continuous predictors has a univariate odds ratio per standard deviation that differs from 1.0 with p<0.05, with feature_011 being the largest (OR/SD < 0.90) and feature_084 being the smallest (OR/SD ~ 1.02).

analyses.code: for c in top_cont: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(zscore(df[c]))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OR per SD (univariate logistic on standardized predictor): feature_011 0.856 (p=7.4e-35), feature_006 0.897 (p=1.2e-19), feature_063 0.938 (p=2.8e-6), feature_092 0.946 (p=1.9e-5), feature_099 1.059 (p=1.4e-6), feature_084 1.024 (p=0.048). Magnitudes ranked as predicted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 acts as a strict ordinal predictor: a model treating feature_057 as a single linear term (per-level log-odds change) fits the data essentially as well as a model treating it as an unordered 3-level categorical, indicating the log-odds gap from level 0 to 1 equals the gap from 1 to 2.

analyses.code: Compare LL of logistic with feature_057 as int vs as 2 dummies (LR test, df=1)
analyses.result_summary: Linear-ordinal feature_057 coefficient = -0.382 per level (OR 0.682, p=2.8e-96). LR test categorical vs linear: chi-square 0.00, df=1, p=0.987. Linear ordinal form is essentially equivalent: each step increases log-odds of non-response by ~0.38, multiplicatively reducing odds of response by ~32%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious multivariable logistic model containing feature_057 (ordinal), feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, feature_084, feature_035, feature_093, feature_121, and feature_014 fits the data substantially better than the null (LLR p < 1e-150) and explains roughly 2% of pseudo-R-squared variation in response.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit() with all 11 predictors plus feature_057 dummies
analyses.result_summary: Final model: pseudo-R2 0.0188, LLR chi-square 853, p<1e-170, AIC 44623. All listed predictors retained nominal significance: feature_057_1 OR 0.681 (p=3e-51), feature_057_2 OR 0.464 (p=3e-77), feature_011 OR 0.96/unit (p=9e-35), feature_006 OR 0.994/unit (p=7e-20), feature_035 OR 1.43 (p=2e-17), feature_099 OR 1.13/unit (p=1e-6), feature_063 OR 0.993/unit (p=1.5e-6), feature_092 OR 0.998/unit (p=1e-5), feature_093 OR 1.13 (p=4e-3), feature_014 OR 1.06 (p=0.02), feature_121 OR 0.92 (p=0.05), feature_084 OR 1.001 (p=0.03). Modest absolute discriminative ability, consistent with a noisy multifactorial outcome.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the top continuous predictors (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099) and feature_057, the only binary features that retain p < 0.05 are feature_035, feature_093, feature_014, and feature_121; the remaining 76 binary features have no adjusted association with response.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(sub[[c]+adjust_full])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted screen of all 80 binary features after biology adjustment. Only four remained at p<0.05: feature_035 (coef +0.360, OR 1.43, p=2e-17), feature_093 (+0.125, OR 1.13, p=3.6e-3), feature_014 (+0.058, OR 1.06, p=1.8e-2), feature_121 (-0.092, OR 0.91, p=4.0e-2). Hypothesis confirmed; the binary predictor universe is dominated by feature_035.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There are no statistically significant pairwise interactions among the top continuous predictors (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099) and feature_035 on response (LR tests with single product term, p < 0.05 threshold).

analyses.code: for (a,b) in pairs: LR test of base model + a*b interaction term
analyses.result_summary: LR-test p-values for interaction product terms (added to base model with main effects): feature_011 x feature_006 p=0.11; feature_011 x feature_063 p=0.076; feature_011 x feature_092 p=0.53; feature_011 x feature_099 p=0.13; feature_006 x feature_063 p=0.65; feature_006 x feature_099 p=0.82; feature_011 x feature_035 p=0.25; feature_006 x feature_035 p=0.30; feature_099 x feature_035 p=0.025. Eight of nine pairwise interactions non-significant; only feature_099 x feature_035 nominally significant (negative).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When a binary indicator for feature_011 == 0 is added to a model that already contains a linear term for feature_011, the indicator is not significant at p<0.05, supporting a single linear effect rather than a separate threshold at zero.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[['feature_011','f011_zero','feature_006','feature_063','feature_092','feature_099','feature_035'] + f57_dums])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Linear feature_011 coefficient = -0.045 (p=8e-27); f011_zero indicator coefficient = -0.064 (p=0.058). Threshold indicator does not reach 0.05; the linear term captures the bulk of the signal. (However, the borderline negative sign on the indicator hints the zero subgroup is slightly *less* likely to respond than its position on the linear continuum predicts, opposite to the raw ORR-vs-quintile pattern.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_035 positive treatment-like effect on objective_response is materially smaller in patients with high feature_099 than in patients with low feature_099 (heterogeneity by feature_099 above vs below median).

analyses.code: Stratify on feature_099 < median vs >= median; compute feature_035 effect within each stratum
analyses.result_summary: Below-median feature_099: feature_035=1 ORR 0.221 vs feature_035=0 ORR 0.157, diff +6.4 ppt (p=7e-12). Above-median feature_099: feature_035=1 ORR 0.221 vs feature_035=0 ORR 0.172, diff +5.0 ppt (p=4e-8). Small attenuation in the high-feature_099 group; consistent in direction with the borderline interaction term in iteration 20 but absolute difference between subgroups is only 1.4 ppt.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_035 effect on objective_response is consistent across self-reported race groups (feature_005); there is no significant feature_035 x feature_005 interaction.

analyses.code: LR test for feature_035 x race interaction in logistic regression of y on feature_035, race dummies, and product terms
analyses.result_summary: Within-race feature_035 effects (absolute difference in ORR): white +6.5 ppt (p=2e-15), black +4.1 (p=0.030), hispanic +4.1 (p=0.014), asian +4.0 (p=0.15), other +5.4 (p=0.27). LR test of full interaction: chi-square 2.06, df=4, p=0.73. Effect is positive in all five groups; no formal heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_125 (5-level: 0/1/2/3/4) shows a weak negative ordinal trend with objective_response, with the highest level having ~2 ppt lower ORR than the lowest level.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_125')['objective_response'].mean(); sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[['feature_125']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_125: level 0=0.174 (n=22643), level 1=0.164, level 2=0.167, level 3=0.168, level 4=0.157 (n=1469). Linear-ordinal coefficient -0.023 per level (p=0.038). Weak signal; non-monotonic at intermediate levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_035 effect on objective_response is consistent in direction across all three feature_057 strata (no significant feature_035 x feature_057 interaction), with absolute ORR differences in the 4-7 percentage-point range in each stratum.

analyses.code: df.pivot_table('objective_response','feature_035','feature_057', aggfunc='mean'); LR test of feature_035 x feature_057 in logistic
analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_035 x feature_057: f057=0 (f035=0: 0.208, f035=1: 0.270, diff +6.2 ppt, n_pos=1331); f057=1 (0.151 vs 0.205, diff +5.4 ppt, n_pos=1777); f057=2 (0.109 vs 0.153, diff +4.4 ppt, n_pos=516). LR test of interaction: chi-square 0.18, df=2, p=0.92. Effect is consistent in direction and similar in magnitude across all biology strata.
